title: euphorbia rohlenae
intro: Euphorbia rohlenae, also known as Rohlena's spurge, is a succulent plant species native to the Czech Republic and Slovakia. It contains various compounds, including euphorbon, which possess medicinal properties and can be used to treat conditions such as inflammation and pain. The plant's extracts have been reported to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, offering potential health benefits in the management of wounds, infections, and other inflammatory disorders. Euphorbia rohlenae is sometimes used in herbal preparations, including tinctures and infusions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety in human use.
